trees.label: The Black Family Tree

nodes.text: The seventies weren't very kind to unwed teen mothers, so her brother took in her son.
Verne Williams is my invention! There used to be a Coast Guard base near Puget sound and many Quileute women married Americans.

trees.notes: The Black family tree has some edits!

trees.label: Clearwater Family

nodes.text: Miles Fuller died in a car accident, and his sister took in his son.

trees.notes:
I entirely made up Dema Clearwater, but I saw the name in one of my research books and decided  it would be nice to have a common ancestress for the Clearwater family. No, she did not have a husband, and no one knows who fathered her kids (and there's a high chance her kids are actually half-siblings).

I also invented a few more branches and siblings, which I find personally fun. And then I filled in some gaps as far as who Brady Fuller's parent is, and how Emily is related to Leah. Had to google what second cousins look like on a tree

trees.label: Uley Family Tree

nodes.text: In this universe, Sam has three younger half-siblings, and only one is like, confirmed.  But he considers them all family.

trees.notes:
The Uley family includes both parts of the Clearwater family and the Lahote family, as they're all related. 

One of the challenges here is to fill in the gaps for Paul's family, as he's descended from Ruth and Gene Lahote, but there's a two generation gap there. So I'm slowly filling that in. The circumstances there are going to be interesting, because I'm certain it's either mentioned or heavily implied that Paul's home life is questionable.

I also took some liberties with Sam's history- his parents are divorced, and based on the tree, you can maybe guess why. In Dreaming, Joshua Uley has one confirmed daughter outside of his marriage and two suspected daughters. I also included Sam's second cousins, the Zhelenov/Grays. The Zelenovs are of Russian descent, as a call back to the Russian ship that probably made first contact with the Quileute, and then I decided I was going to give Sam more family.

Your author note here. Italics and bold work.

A pull quote.

sectionIntros.tree: This is my section for family trees. I owe a great deal to the twilight wiki, twilight lexicon, and this one user on Reddit who connected all these family trees with painstaking effort (https://www.reddit.com/r/twilight/comments/mw2tr0/i_connected_all_the_quileute_family_trees_zoom_in/#lightbox). I've detailed the changes made to each family and why via notes! I hope you enjoy, these are canon for Dreaming!

casting.directorNote: Hey! Thanks for the shout-out! Also, I may be misremembering but I THINK you said you wanted a fancast for Jay? I'm not sure, I may be misremembering. But if so, Archie Madekwe as Kofun in See (tv series) is not a bad one. In seasons one and two he had long hair so I just thought I'd throw it out there IN CASE you were looking for actors. Also, he is VERY tall.
casting.authorNote:
At the time, I actually wasn't looking for fancasts, primarily because I didn't know who to ask. I'm notoriously bad at choosing actors/models/individuals for fancasts, so I stick to picrews and meiker.io. But genuinely, I'm so grateful. Your ideas for the characters have allowed me to imagine them better as well.

In any case, tallness is very important. I love Archie for this, and everything about him is SO JAY. From his dark eyes to general ambiance, I can't see Jay looking like anyone else.

castingCredit: Credits to PinkSparkleDays for the wonderful casting- I could not have come up with any of these on my own and it's entirely their credit!
charCredit: Credits to PinkSparkleDays for the wonderful casting- I could not have come up with any of these on my own and it's entirely their credit!
